pokemon:carvanha.name: Carvanha
pokemon:carvanha.summary: If anything invades Carvanha’s territory, it will swarm and tear at the intruder with its pointed fangs. On its own, however, this Pokémon turns suddenly timid.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: If anything invades Carvanha’s territory, it will swarm and tear at the intruder with its pointed fangs. On its own, however, this Pokémon turns suddenly timid.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
CARVANHA attack ships in swarms, making them sink. Although it is said to be a very vicious POKéMON, it timidly flees as soon as it finds itself alone.
It lives in massive rivers that course through jungles. It swarms prey that enter its territory.
They form packs to attack boats and rip out their hulls to sink them. They live in rivers in the jungle.
It lives in massive rivers that course through jungles. It swarms prey that enter its territory.
If they scent the faintest trace of blood, they rush to attack en masse. When alone, they’re rather cowardly.
Carvanha’s strongly developed jaws and its sharply pointed fangs pack the destructive power to rip out boat hulls. Many boats have been attacked and sunk by this Pokémon.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
CARVANHA’s strongly developed jaws and its sharply pointed fangs pack the destructive power to rip out boat hulls. Many boats have been attacked and sunk by this POKéMON.
If anything invades CARVANHA’s territory, it will swarm and tear at the intruder with its pointed fangs. On its own, however, this POKéMON turns suddenly timid.
These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.
They form packs to attack boats and rip out their hulls to sink them. They live in rivers in the jungle.
Each school has its own territory. Any intruders are mercilessly attacked with fangs bared.
It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.
With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.
They’re such cowards that they won’t hunt alone. When five or so of them get together, they suddenly turn ferocious!
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
They swarm any foe that invades their territory. Their sharp fangs can tear out boat hulls.
They form packs to attack boats and rip out their hulls to sink them. They live in rivers in the jungle.
It lives in massive rivers that course through jungles. It swarms prey that enter its territory.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: If anything invades Carvanha’s territory, it will swarm and tear at the intruder with its pointed fangs. On its own, however, this Pokémon turns suddenly timid.

pokemon:sharpedo.name: Sharpedo
pokemon:sharpedo.summary: Sharpedo can swim at speeds of up to 75 mph by jetting seawater out of its backside. This Pokémon’s drawback is its inability to swim long distances.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Sharpedo can swim at speeds of up to 75 mph by jetting seawater out of its backside. This Pokémon’s drawback is its inability to swim long distances.
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
The vicious and sly gangster of the sea. Its skin is specially textured to minimize drag in water. Its speed tops out at over 75 miles per hour.
The ruffian of the seas, it has fangs that crunch through iron. It swims by jetting water from its rear.
It can swim at speeds of 75 mph by jetting seawater through its body. It is the bandit of the sea.
The ruffian of the seas, it has fangs that crunch through iron. It swims by jetting water from its rear.
It has a sad history. In the past, its dorsal fin was a treasured foodstuff, so this Pokémon became a victim of overfishing.
Nicknamed “the bully of the sea,” Sharpedo is widely feared. Its cruel fangs grow back immediately if they snap off. Just one of these Pokémon can thoroughly tear apart a supertanker.
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Nicknamed “the bully of the sea,” SHARPEDO is widely feared. Its cruel fangs grow back immediately if they snap off. Just one of these POKéMON can thoroughly tear apart a supertanker.
SHARPEDO can swim at speeds of up to 75 mph by jetting seawater out of its backside. This POKéMON’s drawback is its inability to swim long distances.
This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sea. Any ship entering the waters Sharpedo calls home will be attacked—no exceptions.
It can swim at speeds of 75 mph by jetting seawater through its body. It is the bandit of the sea.
It pursues its prey at speeds of 75 mph and finishes them off with fangs that can crush iron. It is known as the bully of the sea.
As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.
It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It’s very sensitive to the scent of blood.
People believe that carrying one of its discarded fangs will prevent mishaps at sea, so the fangs are made into accessories.
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
Its fangs rip through sheet iron. It swims at 75 mph and is known as “The Bully of the Sea.”
It can swim at speeds of 75 mph by jetting seawater through its body. It is the bandit of the sea.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Sharpedo can swim at speeds of up to 75 mph by jetting seawater out of its backside. This Pokémon’s drawback is its inability to swim long distances.
